summary: Solid soundboard capture (with an out‑of‑phase quirk) of a memorable 1993 show featuring Ornette Coleman, a scorching Johnny B. Goode, The Other One and a hall‑stomping Lovelight.
review: The recording is a generally clear DAT‑SBD source, though the encore drops to a lower‑grade SBD and one channel is out of phase, which can be fixed by inverting it. Musically the night shines thanks to Ornette’s guest spot, Jerry’s fiery Wolf/Johnny B. Goode work and a powerful second set that builds from a slow start into an explosive Other One and a standout Lovelight, while some moments (Mardi Gras Drums>Iko, Corrina) fall flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews praise several highlight performances and a good‑quality soundboard source but note recording phase issues and uneven set moments, leading to an overall above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp DAT‑SBD capture; encore sourced from a lower‑grade SBD. One reviewer notes the mix is out of phase (invert one channel fixes it). No major dropouts reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls smoothly and ‘smokes’ from start to finish. The second set begins slowly, gains momentum with Ornette’s free‑jazz challenge, erupts into a spacey Playin' that leads into an intense Other One, and closes on a high‑energy Lovelight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia rides the night hard – Wolf all night, a killer Johnny B. Goode, and pushes harder when Ornette challenges him; some note his health may be limiting his usual magic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s lead‑bass tone is loud, bouncy and takes charge during Playin' and Broken Arrow, earning praise as the ‘Phil save’ of the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mardi Gras Drums>Iko described as boring/plodding, but the later drum segment with a didgeridoo and Ornette’s contribution is highlighted as cool.
